calendar.matters:
Petition To Terminate Irrevocable Trust
Off Calendar Mar-11-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Hespenheide for (petitioners) Tim Zhang & James Cheung.

The Court stated that it was trying to figure in any additional points. Ms. Hespenheide addressed the examiner's notes stated that the House trust did not get funded but is now rectified, that the main underlying reason is a practical one, that Ted Cheung had a right to amend the survivors trust but not the bypass trust to provide 50% interest in the Cabrillo property to the daughters of a previous marriage, that the litigation was resolved, that the Cabrillo Street property was to be and has been sold, that the 50% for the Survivor's trust has been distributed, that the shares for the Bypass trust are held in a bank account, that they have not been distributed or reinvested and that they would like to invest in potential housing for themselves. The Court asked what would happen if it does not grant the petition. Ms. Hespenheide responded that they will have to keep everything in the irrevocable trust, that the income is mandated to be distributed, that the principal could be distributed and that two signatures are needed on everything which makes it burdensome. The Court asked what would happen if it is of the view that the principal needs to be intact. Ms. Hespenheide stated that they are not sure they see the need to maintain the principal and that they have enough confidence in the trustees to serve together or alone. The Court took the petition to determine irrevocable trust under submission and stated that if there is an alternative proposal it will consider it at that time.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
